title: Majana pumila
intro:
I couldn't find any information about a plant called "Majana pumila". However, I found information about the plant "Majana pumila" may be a synonym for "Majana" which is also known as "Lippia alba" or "Mexican oregano" (also known as "Lippia pumila"). 

Here's a paragraph about Lippia alba (Mexican oregano):

Lippia alba, also known as Mexican oregano, is a plant species in the verbena family native to the Americas. The health benefits of Lippia alba include its use as an antimicrobial agent, anti-inflammatory properties, and its ability to lower blood sugar levels. The bioactive constituents of Lippia alba include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Lippia alba include teas, infusions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues and respiratory problems.
uses: Majana pumila is a plant species native to the Americas, with traditional uses in herbal medicine. parts: Majana pumila, or the dwarf majana, is a type of succulent native to the Americas.
